title: Prefetch, Consumer Concurrency, Capacity, and Fairness · Contract Lens: Fair Tenant Dispatch
summary: A consumer sets prefetch zero for maximum speed, starts unbounded promises, ignores message bytes and dependency pools, and scales consumers from queue depth alone. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fair tenant dispatch increment in prefetch, concurrency, and capacity budget model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence.
Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Fair Tenant Dispatch · Theory Fair Tenant Dispatch
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9821 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine prefetch, active handlers, downstream capacity, and message-byte budgets; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence.
